examples: A single e-scooter visible just below the surface.
A single plastic bottle drifting along the embankment.
Shopping cart half-submerged near the bridge piling.
Unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow.
Surface entirely covered in green-blue scum — full bloom.
Dense surface mat covering roughly a third of the visible water.
Patches of greenish water near the houseboat moorings.
Iridescent rainbow sheen across a moderate area near a moored boat.

narrative.intro: Nine approved reports this week, up from zero the previous period. The dominant pattern is a mix of surface debris and significant algal blooms, with the Prinsengracht seeing the most activity.

by_type.trash: Four reports across the Prinsengracht, Grimburgwal, and Krom Boomssloot — mostly small litter like plastic bottles, though a couple of submerged e-scooters and a shopping cart were noted.
by_type.other: One report from the Prinsengracht describing unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow, a concerning observation.
by_type.blooming: Three reports from the Prinsengracht, Snoekjesgracht, and Rokin; some areas are seeing dense green-blue scum and surface mats, indicating a severe bloom in some spots.
by_type.petroleum: A single iridescent rainbow sheen was spotted near a moored boat on the Prinsengracht, which appears to be a minor incident.

narrative.outlook: Temperatures remain cool, with highs peaking at 15.9 °C and lows dipping as low as 4.8 °C. These conditions should slow or pause the expansion of the algal blooms reported this week.
narrative.thanks: Thank you to the nine citizens who shared their photos and reports. Your vigilance keeps the water clear and the city's canals are better for it. To everyone reading: if you spot something unusual, please send us the next report. 💧
